title: veronica schmidtiana subsp. yezo-alpina
intro: Veronica schmidtiana subsp. yezo-alpina, also known as the Yezo alpine veronica, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the Veronica genus. It is native to Japan and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health conditions. The plant contains iridoid glycosides, which have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its health benefits, including reducing stress, improving sleep, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the Veronica schmidtiana subsp. yezo-alpina are typically dried and used as infusions, teas, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
